free_text: Measurement and analysis of alpha-induced reactions
on Ta,Ag and Co
The total alpha-particle flux was measured with the
help of the ORTEC current integrator.
114 cm3 Ge(Li) or HPGe detectors.
The efficiency calibrations of the detectors were made
with a calibrated Eu-152 source.
Total error from:
The error due to target foil thickness.
The error due to beam current.
The uncertainty due to the detector
efficiency.
The error due to the analysis of the gamma-
ray spectra.
The uncertainties caused by the larger
dimensions of the irradiation area and the edge
effects contribute a 5% error to the average
error of the alpha-induced reaction.
The errors of the thickness of the target
foils, energy straggling as well as beam energy
resolution.
The hybrid model of Blann.
SD:Updated to new date formats, lower case;
corrected according to last EXFOR rules and Dict.
FACILITY code and author's name were corrected. Small
corrections in all Subents. DATA-ERR -> ERR-T.
STATUS was added. BIB updated. Subent 003 was split.
Subent 006 was added.
SD: Corrections in all Subents. Several
Subents were split according to EXFOR rules.
Tantalum stacks of about 10 to 20 foils with thickness
25 um, were used.
Tbl. 2
SD: MONITOR was deleted. SF5=(M) added
to REACTION (1) code.
SD: Subent was split in four (see
Subents 007-009).
Silver stacks of about 10 to 20 foils with thickness
25 um, were used.
Tbl. 3
SD: MONITOR was deleted. Subent was split
to two. Part of data was moved to Subent 006.
SD: SF4=In-11g -> In-111 in REACTION code.
STATUS updated.
- absolute
cross-sections from this Subent agree with the reported
values of Michel and Brinkmann (1980) within the quoted
error in the overlapping region of energies.
Tantalum stacks of about 10 to 20 foils with thickness
50 um, were used.
Tbl. 4
SD: MONITOR was deleted;
MONIT-REF -> REL-REF. SF3 -> X in all REACTION codes.
SD: Subent was split in five (see
Subents 010-013).
Aluminium - flux monitor foils were added in front
of most of the stacks.
Since the reaction 27Al(alpha,x) 24Na had been studied
extensively in the past, authors have checked the
quality of their absolute cross-section values by
measuring the cross-sections for this reaction by
way of flux monitoring foils kept in front of some of
the stacks.
A comparison of the production cross-section for 24Na
from alpha-bombardment with other authors shows
good agreement (see REL-REF).
Text (p204)

Note from N.Otsuka: Caption of fig.8 (see PRM,30,193 )
is incorrect because 109Ag(alpha,x)105Ag
threshold is ~65 MeV (should be 107Ag(alpha,x))
